skill.name: claude-code-guide
skill.description: Master guide for using Claude Code effectively. Includes configuration templates, prompting strategies, debugging techniques, and best practices for AI-assisted development.
skill.summary: Comprehensive guide for Claude Code with templates and best practices
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 15 potential issues across documentation file, all confirmed as false positives. Shell command patterns are markdown code examples in documentation templates. GitHub URL reference is documentation attribution. Cryptographic algorithm flags are false positives from the word 'thinking' in prompts. No executable code present. This is a safe documentation skill providing configuration templates and best practices for Claude Code.

low_findings.title: Documentation Shell Commands
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected shell command patterns in markdown code blocks at SKILL.md lines 14, 16, 20-27, 38-41, 64. These are documentation examples showing npm commands like 'npm run dev' and 'npm test' in configuration templates. No executable code present.

low_findings.title: Documentation URL Reference
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected hardcoded URL at SKILL.md line 70. This is a GitHub attribution link to the original guide source. No network requests or code execution.

low_findings.title: False Positive Cryptography Flags
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'thinking' keywords as weak cryptography at SKILL.md lines 3, 59, 73. These are prompt engineering suggestions ('Think step-by-step', 'forget previous instructions') for AI interactions. No cryptographic functionality present.

content.user_title: Master Claude Code with Essential Configuration Templates
content.value_statement: Claude Code requires proper setup to be effective. This skill provides ready-to-use configuration templates and proven prompting strategies that maximize AI-assisted development productivity.
actual_capabilities: Provides ready-to-use CLAUDE.md configuration templates for new projects
Teaches thinking keywords that improve AI reasoning quality
Offers debugging strategies when Claude Code behaves unexpectedly
Documents best practices for iterative AI-assisted development
Includes workflow guidance for effective human-AI collaboration
limitations: This is a documentation-only skill with no executable code
Cannot automatically configure your project - you must apply templates manually
Does not interact with Claude Code directly or modify settings
Effectiveness depends on your willingness to follow documented practices

use_cases.title: New Project Setup
use_cases.description: Developers starting a new project use the CLAUDE.md template to establish consistent guidelines for Claude Code, ensuring the AI understands project structure, commands, and coding standards from day one.

use_cases.title: Team Standardization
use_cases.description: Development teams adopt the configuration templates and best practices to create consistent AI-assisted workflows across all members, improving collaboration and code quality.

use_cases.title: Troubleshooting AI Interactions
use_cases.description: When Claude Code produces unexpected results or gets stuck, developers use the debugging techniques and thinking keywords to guide the AI back to productive behavior.
use_cases.target_user: Users experiencing issues with AI coding assistants

prompt_templates.title: Basic Configuration Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a CLAUDE.md file for my React TypeScript project using the claude-code-guide template
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new project and want to configure Claude Code properly

prompt_templates.title: Debugging Assistance
prompt_templates.prompt: The agent is making the same mistake repeatedly. Use the claude-code-guide debugging strategies to help it understand the issue
prompt_templates.scenario: Claude Code is stuck in a loop or producing incorrect results

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Reasoning Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the 'Think step-by-step' and 'Verify your assumptions' keywords from the claude-code-guide when analyzing this bug
prompt_templates.scenario: Need deeper analysis for complex debugging tasks

prompt_templates.title: Workflow Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the claude-code-guide best practices and suggest how I can improve my current AI-assisted development workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Want to maximize productivity with Claude Code

output_examples.input: Help me set up CLAUDE.md for my Node.js project
output_examples.output: Creates a structured markdown file with sections for project commands, code style guidelines, and workflow instructions based on the template

output_examples.input: The agent keeps suggesting the wrong approach
output_examples.output: Provides specific debugging steps including clearing context, using explicit instructions, and adding lessons to CLAUDE.md

output_examples.input: What are the best practices for working with Claude Code?
output_examples.output: Lists key practices: use small focused contexts instead of dumping entire codebase, iterate with small changes and verification, and correct mistakes immediately

best_practices: Always create a CLAUDE.md file in new project roots using the provided template
Use thinking keywords like 'Think step-by-step' and 'Analyze the root cause' when requesting complex analysis
Start new sessions if Claude Code becomes confused or stuck in incorrect patterns
anti_patterns: Dumping entire codebase into context - use grep or find to locate relevant files first
Making large changes without verification - iterate with small changes and test after each step
Being vague about paths or filenames - provide specific absolute paths and exact filenames

faq.question: What is CLAUDE.md and why do I need it?
faq.answer: CLAUDE.md is a configuration file you create in your project root that guides Claude Code. It contains your project commands, code style preferences, and workflow instructions so the AI understands how to work with your specific project.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without Claude Code?
faq.answer: The templates and practices are designed specifically for Claude Code but many principles like clear project guidelines and iterative development apply to other AI coding assistants as well.

faq.question: What are thinking keywords?
faq.answer: Thinking keywords are phrases like 'Think step-by-step', 'Analyze the root cause', and 'Verify your assumptions' that you can include in your prompts to trigger deeper reasoning from the AI agent.

faq.question: How do I fix Claude Code when it gets stuck?
faq.answer: Start by clearing context with a new session or asking the agent to forget previous instructions. Then provide extremely specific instructions about paths, filenames, and desired outcomes. Request verbose output to diagnose issues.

faq.question: Why does the guide recommend small contexts instead of showing the entire codebase?
faq.answer: Large contexts can overwhelm the AI and reduce quality. Using grep or find to locate relevant files first keeps the focus tight and improves analysis accuracy for the specific task at hand.

faq.question: How often should I update my CLAUDE.md file?
faq.answer: Update it whenever your project structure changes, new commands are added, or you notice Claude Code repeatedly making mistakes that could be prevented with better documentation.
